text.div:
Generated Narrative: StructureDefinition EHDSDischargeReport

NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
EHDSDischargeReport 0..*EHDSDocumentDischarge Report model

Elements defined in Ancestors:header, header, presentedForm
Instances of this logical model can be the target of a Reference
header
intendedRecipient[x] 0..*Intended recipient
intendedRecipientEHDSPatientEHDSPatient
intendedRecipientEHDSRelatedPersonEHDSRelatedPerson
intendedRecipientEHDSHealthProfessionalEHDSHealthProfessional
intendedRecipientEHDSOrganisationEHDSOrganisation
intendedRecipientEHDSDeviceEHDSDevice
healthInsuranceAndPaymentInformation 0..*EHDSCoverageHealth insurance and payment information
body 0..1BaseStructured body of the discharge report document
alerts 0..1BaseSection: Alerts.
narrative 0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the section
medicalAlert 0..*EHDSAlertDescription of medical alerts in textual format: any clinical information that is imperative to know so that the life or health of the patient does not come under threat.
encounterInformation 1..1BaseSection: Encounter information.
narrative 1..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the section
encounter 0..1EHDSEncounterEncounter information
admissionEvaluation 0..1BaseSection: Admission evaluation. Admission evaluation section should be reported only if it is relevant to ensure continuity of care.
narrative 0..1stringNarrative content of the section. This narrative shell containing either summary narrative description of all subsections, or similar narrative sub-section elements should be provided.
objectiveFindings 0..*EHDSObservationObjective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
functionalStatus 0..*EHDSFunctionalStatusAn individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
note 0..1stringFree text notes by the evaluation performer
patientHistory 0..1BaseSection: Patient health history (anamnesis) containing only information that is highly relevant for this specific episode of care.
narrative 0..1stringNarrative content of the section. This narrative shell containing either summary narrative description of all subsections, or similar narrative subsection elements should be provided.
note 0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
courseOfEncounter 0..1BaseCourse of inpatient or outpatient encounter.
diagnosticSummary 0..*EHDSConditionAll problems/diagnoses that affect care during the inpatient case or are important to be recorded to ensure continuity of care. The diagnostic summary differentiates, in accordance with the international recommendation, between problems treated during hospital stay and other (untreated) problems. Treated problems are problems that were the subject of diagnostics, therapy, nursing, or (continuous) monitoring during the hospitalisation. Furthermore problems could be divided into three categories: problems present on admission (POA), conditions acquired during hospital stay (HAC) and problems that cannot be classified as being of any of the two (N/A). The diagnostic summary contains all conditions as they were recognised at the end of hospitalisation, after all examinations. This section contains concise, well specified, codeable, summary of problems. Problems are ordered by importance (main problems first) during hospital stay. Description of the problem might be completed with additional details in the medical history section and/or in the Synthesis section.
significantProcedures 0..*EHDSProcedureSignificant procedures - procedures performed during encounter which are significant for continuity of care, e.g. surgeries and other instrumental interventions (endoscopic, intravascular), chemotherapy, radiotherapy, purification methods (dialysis, hemoperfusion), circulation support methods (counterpulsation, etc.), administration of blood derivatives or others. This section does not include purely diagnostic procedures (MRI, CT, etc.).
medicalDevicesAndImplants 0..*EHDSDeviceUseImplants and used medical devices that affected or may affect the provision of health services (diagnosis and treatment). Also medical devices explanted, or which use was stopped during encounter.
pharmacotherapy 0..*EHDSMedicationStatementSelected drug treatment during encounter. Medicinal products that were administered during encounter and whose administration has already been discontinued before discharge. Only products which are important for continuity of care (antibiotics other than completely routine, corticosteroids in high doses, etc.) should be listed. Products which administration will continue after discharge will be also recorder in the Medication summary section. Medicinal products, the administration of which was started during encounter, but is also recommended after discharge, will be listed in medication summary.
significantObservationResults[x] 0..*Results of significant functional, diagnostic (including laboratory results), and imaging examinations to ensure continuity of care, performed during encounter. Results of examinations ordered but not yet delivered should be presented separately from results already delivered.
significantObservationResultsEHDSObservationEHDSObservation
significantObservationResultsEHDSLaboratoryObservationEHDSLaboratoryObservation
note 0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
dischargeDetails 0..1BaseDischarge details
narrative 0..1stringNarrative content of the section. This narrative shell containing either summary narrative description of all subsections, or similar narrative sub-section elements should be provided.
objectiveFindings 0..*EHDSObservationObjective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
functionalStatus 0..*EHDSFunctionalStatusAn individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
note 0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
medicationSummary 0..*BaseMedication summary. Summary information on the medication recommended for the period after discharge, indicating whether the medication is changed or newly started. Compared to previous practices, the overview is supplemented with medication that has been discontinued.
narrative 0..1stringNarrative content of the section.
medicationUse 1..1EHDSMedicationStatementDetails about medication and dosaging
carePlan 0..1BaseCare plan and other recommendations after discharge.
narrative 0..1stringNarrative content of the section.
carePlan 0..*EHDSCarePlanStructured care plan after discharge. Multiple care plans could be provided.
otherRecommendations 0..1stringOther recommendations (advice) after discharge. E.g., recommendation to suggest hip replacement, reduce number of cigarettes, stop smoking, increase physical exercises, etc.
synthesis 0..1stringClinical synthesis (e.g. description of reasons and course of encounter) clustered by managed conditions. Clinical synthesis may include clinical reasoning (differential diagnostics, explanation of clinical context) in clinically complex conditions.
attachments[x] 0..*Report attachments data elements
attachmentsEHDSAttachmentEHDSAttachment
attachmentsEHDSMediaEHDSMedia

Documentation for this format

name: EHDSDischargeReport
title: Discharge Report model
description: A generic, flexible model for any kind of discharge report. Different types of encounters may require adding relevant sections and elements, or omitting irrelevant ones, depending on their data needs.

element.short: Discharge Report model
element.definition: A generic, flexible model for any kind of discharge report. Different types of encounters may require adding relevant sections and elements, or omitting irrelevant ones, depending on their data needs.

element.short: Document header elements
element.definition: Common header for all patient-related data

element.short: Patient/subject information
element.definition: Patient/subject information

element.short: Unique identifier of the document
element.definition: Business identifier for the object

element.short: Resource authoring details
element.definition: Resource authoring details

element.short: Author(s) by whom the resource was/were authored. Multiple authors could be provided.
element.definition: Author(s) by whom the resource was/were authored. Multiple authors could be provided.

element.short: Date and time of authoring/issuing
element.definition: Date and time of authoring/issuing

element.short: Date and time of the last update to the resource (may be used for technical corrections).
element.definition: Date and time of the last update to the resource (may be used for technical corrections).

element.short: Status of the resource or document
element.definition: Status of the resource or document

element.short: Reason for the current status of the resource.
element.definition: Reason for the current status of the resource.

element.short: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.
element.definition: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

element.short: Type of document at hand, e.g. 60591-5 Patient summary document.
element.definition: Type of document at hand, e.g. 60591-5 Patient summary document.

element.short: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'
element.definition: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'

element.short: Time of service that is being documented
element.definition: Time of service that is being documented

element.short: Business version of the document.
element.definition: Business version of the document.

element.short: Document attestation details
element.definition: Document attestation details

element.short: Attester who validated the document. Multiple attesters could be provided.
element.definition: Attester who validated the document. Multiple attesters could be provided.

element.short: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester.
element.definition: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester.

element.short: Document legal authentication details

element.short: The person or organization taking responsibility for the medical content of the document

element.short: Date and time when the document was authorised.
element.definition: Date and time when the document was authorised.

element.short: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.
element.definition: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

element.short: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)
element.definition: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)

element.short: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.
element.definition: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.

element.short: Level of confidentiality of the document. Implicit value is normal.
element.definition: Level of confidentiality of the document. Implicit value is normal.

element.short: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document
element.definition: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document

element.short: Intended recipient
element.definition: Information recipient (intended recipient or recipients of the report, additional recipients might be identified by the ordering party, e.g. GP, other specialist), if applicable

element.short: All problems/diagnoses that affect care during the inpatient case or are important to be recorded to ensure continuity of care. The diagnostic summary differentiates, in accordance with the international recommendation, between problems treated during hospital stay and other (untreated) problems. Treated problems are problems that were the subject of diagnostics, therapy, nursing, or (continuous) monitoring during the hospitalisation. Furthermore problems could be divided into three categories: problems present on admission (POA), conditions acquired during hospital stay (HAC) and problems that cannot be classified as being of any of the two (N/A). The diagnostic summary contains all conditions as they were recognised at the end of hospitalisation, after all examinations. This section contains concise, well specified, codeable, summary of problems. Problems are ordered by importance (main problems first) during hospital stay. Description of the problem might be completed with additional details in the medical history section and/or in the Synthesis section.

This section does not include purely diagnostic procedures (MRI, CT, etc.).
